items.briefing:
Ransomware targeting financial organisations is on the rise, with supplier and third-party relationships flagged as a growing entry point for attackers. Separately, the China-linked threat group TA4922 has expanded its cybercrime activity globally, meaning Australian organisations — including training providers — should be alert to phishing and credential-theft attempts. Cyber insurance may appear more affordable right now, but policies are increasingly excluding common attack types, so review your coverage carefully.

On a positive note, Australia has a narrow window to develop home-grown AI capability securely. **Practical tip:** Check that your cyber insurance policy actually covers ransomware and third-party breaches — don't assume it does.

featured.title: Financial ransomware & supplier risks rise, report warns
featured.quote: Australian financial sector faces critical ransomware threat with supplier vulnerabilities exposing banks and investment firms.
